definitions.technical: A consensus-driven, cryptographically secured database replicated across multiple network participants, where data is shared to create a public record of verified transactions. The GENIUS Act legally defines it as a network where data is shared to create a cryptographically secured digital record.
definitions.lay: A shared digital record book that is copied across many computers in a network. Every copy stays in sync, and the records are protected by cryptography so they cannot be secretly altered. This is the foundational technology behind blockchain.
definitions.parenthetical: (a cryptographically secured, consensus-driven database replicated across multiple network participants)
definitions.legal_significance: The GENIUS Act includes a statutory definition of 'distributed ledger technology,' which determines the technological scope of the regulatory framework. This definition is broader than 'blockchain' and may encompass future ledger architectures.
